items.title: Strait of Hormuz remains blocked by Iran while zombie ships sail through
items.summary: Iran has effectively blocked the only way out of the Persian Gulf, leaving about 3,000 ships stranded. But two zombie ships appear to have sailed in and out of the Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Donald Trump says US and Iran 'want deal' as Tehran calls claim of talks 'fake news'
items.summary: Donald Trump says there have been "major points of agreement" in US-Iran talks to end the war. But Iran denies those claims.

items.title: Buying a share of my in-laws' home helped us into the property market
items.summary: Experts say housing affordability is pushing people to rethink the rule book on property ownership.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
items.summary: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

items.title: Tasmania Devils make their VFL debut
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Water Corporation's revised bridge limits worry South West WA farmers
items.summary: A decision by WA's state-owned water utility to cut weight limits on bridges it controls is causing disruption for farmers and businesses south of Perth.

items.title: St John WA reveals rise in assaults on paramedics as officer details incidents that left her 'traumatised'

items.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Grandfather clock, jewellery box in alleged $148k theft by former National Trust boss
items.summary: Matthew Paul Smithies was charged with the alleged thefts in 2023. Until now, a suppression order has prevented the media from reporting on the case, which remains before the Supreme Court.

items.title: Perth parents frustrated by difficulties accessing specialist epilepsy care for their son
items.summary: A Perth mother whose three-year-old son has a complex form of epilepsy, believes the health system is failing her son because he is being pushed between two public hospitals.

items.title: Farmers warn of food price spikes within weeks as Middle East war continues
items.summary: Australia's peak farming body is warning consumers the price of groceries is about to jump as producers and the freight industry grapple with diesel price hikes and supply issues.

items.title: ASX falls, Asia tumbles, gold crashes as Trump keeps markets on edge
items.summary: The Australian share market fell, Asian shares were generally clobbered and precious metals were a long way off earlier peaks. But it could have been worse.

items.title: Nurses' union claims Tasmanian government policy has contributed to patient deaths
items.summary: The Tasmanian nurses' union says a policy that gives paramedics an hour to transfer the care of their patients to an emergency department has led to patient deaths. The state government says there's no evidence to support the claim.

items.title: Cricket Australia boss Todd Greenberg says New Zealand's BBL rejection is not a surprise
items.summary: New Zealand's rejection of an offer to join the BBL did not surprise Cricket Australia's boss.

items.summary: Constant malfunctions, long repair times and inadequate power supply are causing some EV drivers to rethink long trips in Western Australia.

items.title: Make-ahead breakfast ideas for a stress-free morning routine

items.title: Man who killed partner Nikkita Azzopardi found dead in Melbourne jail cell
items.summary: The grieving family of a Melbourne woman who was bashed to death say they felt relieved when they learned her killer died in jail over the weekend.

items.summary: A man is hospitalised with serious injuries after being hit by a woman who police believe fell from a building in Melbourne's CBD.

items.summary: Staff were informed this morning that a majority of ABC workers had voted against the broadcaster's latest enterprise agreement offer.

items.summary: Jarome Luai will miss a month with his knee injury, but his Wests Tigers halves partner Adam Doueihi has been cleared of a hamstring issue and is on track to play in the club's next game.

items.summary: The state government is proposing to scrap drug diversion laws, which provide a three strikes system before criminal charges, in a crackdown on "addiction and criminal offending".

items.summary: From the players who lit up the tournament to those who never quite got going, here is a closer look at how each Matilda fared at the 2026 Women's Asian Cup.

items.title: How living with a disability in regional Australia poses extra challenges
items.summary: NDIS recipients living in regional Australia are calling for the government to provide better care for those who live remotely with travel costs often eating up a large portion of their support budgets.

items.summary: Energy Minister Chris Bowen has confirmed dozens of petrol stations on Australia's east coast are facing shortages amid a Question Time grilling on fuel supplies.

items.summary: Nine months before the Iran war oil crisis, the federal government was warned of the "fragility" of Australia's critical infrastructure if a fuel crisis took hold.

items.title: Few leaders in Australia are as firmly entrenched in power as Peter Malinauskas
items.summary: After Saturday's landslide, Premier Peter Malinauskas has more political capital than any South Australian Labor leader in modern history.

items.summary: A fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security leads to lengthy queues at US airports.

items.title: Barking dogs, overhanging trees, shared fences — how to resolve issues with your neighbour
items.summary: Someone feeling like their neighbour is intruding on them in "either psychological or practical ways" tends to be at the heart of most disputes. So if you're having an issue with the people next door, what's the best way to approach it?

items.title: Why Brumbies skipper Ryan Lonergan is crucial to his side's Super Rugby Pacific championship hopes
items.summary: Brumbies captain Ryan Lonergan led by example in the come-from-behind win over the Chiefs, underlining how vital he is to his side's finals hopes.

items.title: Private health insurers expand into GP clinics, telehealth, prompting calls for more oversight
items.summary: Increasing partnerships between health insurers, GP centres and telehealth services has raised concerns about a potential two-tier system in which privately insured patients have more affordable access to GP consultations.

items.title: Disbelief and praise from teenagers and parents as Indonesia's social media ban set to take effect
items.summary: In a country with 70 million children, some question how the incoming social media ban in Indonesia will be effectively implemented, while others welcome the new restrictions.

items.title: Shipping boss pushes for new Bridport channel entrance to stop his ship running aground
items.summary: David Harris's ship bottomed out with 400 cattle on board at the end of last year, leading to a 12-hour wait for the next high tide. He wants a solution.

items.title: Why Ghanim named his robotic arms after the Peaky Blinders brothers
items.summary: Ghanim Al Shnen became a pioneer in the use of mind-controlled robotic limbs after losing both his arms. Although he would never have chosen this fate, it ultimately gave him the life for which he longed.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The Matildas need a grand plan to stave off end-of-an-era concerns
items.summary: The Women's Asian Cup has shown that without a grand plan for the future of women's football and a willingness to stick to it, the Matildas may never capture an elusive major trophy again.

items.title: Home building industry facing COVID-like price spikes due to Middle East war
items.summary: From sand to concrete, suppliers are bringing in "emergency fuel levies" as petrol prices soar due to the war in the Middle East. Major companies that supply pipes for plumbing are even warning of shortages.

items.title: South Australian election shows One Nation threatens Liberals and Labor
items.summary: One Nation delivers its best electoral result in nearly 30 years, demonstrating that opinion polls showing its vote rising have not been a political beat-up but a real electoral phenomenon that will lead to a political reckoning in Australian politics.

items.title: Markets are complacent as Iran war threatens global economy
items.summary: The markets' benign assessment of the Iran war is wrong and the damage to the global economy could get worse.

items.title: Microsoft's default wallpaper Bliss, the most viewed photograph in history, turns 30 this year
items.summary: Plastered across screens in offices, schools and homes globally, Bliss is one of the most well-known images in the world.

items.title: Cricket Australia releases full international schedule for the 2026/2027 summer
items.summary: Cricket Australia has been forced into starting the core part of its Test summer in Perth on a Wednesday, as a result of the most congested schedule in the sport's history.

items.summary: The Matildas have backed Caitlin Foord to overcome her Asian Cup heartache as questions continue to swirl about the future of captain Sam Kerr.

items.title: Alana King takes two wickets as Australia defeats West Indies in women's T20 series
items.summary: Alana King adds more intrigue to Australia's spin dilemma, strengthening her T20 World Cup claims with two more wickets to save her side from an upset loss to West Indies.

items.title: Eels coach calls out NRL rules after key forward suffers torn ACL
items.summary: Parramatta Eels forward J'maine Hopgood suffers an ACL injury in a victory over Dragons, leaving coach Jason Ryles to lament the NRL's insistence on speeding up the game.

items.title: Anthony Albanese urges 'vigilance' against those seeking to 'turn back the clock' on Australia
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says the country must be "vigilant" against politicians seeking to return to an era of Australia that was less accepting of migrants, following the South Australian election where One Nation overtook the Liberal Party on a platform opposing multiculturalism.

items.title: Socceroos recall for Awer Mabil sends message ahead of FIFA World Cup
items.summary: The recall of forgotten Socceroo Awer Mabil is proof Tony Popovic places no limit on how wide he casts his FIFA World Cup selection net, says Australia defender Jason Geria.

items.title: Hardcore band Parkway Drive distances itself as former merchandise manager pleads guilty in court
items.summary: Hardcore music megastars Parkway Drive are distancing themselves from their former merchandise manager, who has pleaded guilty to having sexual intercourse with a minor.

items.title: '25 years is long enough': Scott won't ask for patience from Bombers fans
items.summary: West Coast wins its first match since round 11 last year while the Bombers sink to a second straight crushing loss to start the 2026 season.

items.title: When is location sharing a red flag in relationships?

items.title: Trump threatens to put ICE agents in airport security unless Democrats end shutdown
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to deploy ICE agents to US airports if congressional Democrats do not agree to fund airport safety immediately.
